Machine Learning · Mathematical Optimization · Interpretable AI · Trustworthy AI

Sepideh Forouzi

Hi! I'm Sepideh.

I am a machine learning researcher whose work lies at the intersection of data science, artificial intelligence, and mathematical optimization. I hold an MSc in Data Science from the University of Colorado Boulder and a Postgraduate Certificate in Applied AI Solutions Development from George Brown College.

My research lies at the intersection of interpretable AI, neural network theory, optimization, and statistical machine learning. I am particularly interested in developing mathematically grounded learning algorithms that are both theoretically rigorous and practically effective.

Recently, I co-developed Lehmer Activation Units (LAUs), a novel family of interpretable neural activation functions with universal approximation guarantees, published in Entropy (MDPI, 2026). I am also working on optimization-based formulations of neural networks using mixed-integer programming for interpretability, sparsity, and formal verification.

Beyond my research, I enjoy building scalable AI systems spanning machine learning, large language models, time-series analysis, and optimization.

I am currently seeking PhD opportunities beginning in Fall 2027 and welcome opportunities for research collaboration.

Research Experience

Machine Learning Scientist (Co-op)

Jan 2026 &ndash; Apr 2026

Kinectrics, Toronto, Canada

  • Developed and validated LSTM-based deep-learning models for anomaly detection on multivariate wind-farm time series, improving detection performance and reliability relative to baseline methods.
  • Formulated and compared statistical, graph-based, and physics-informed approaches to anomaly detection; performed feature engineering, model validation, and threshold calibration on large-scale temporal data.
  • Integrated trained models into an LLM-based decision-support tool for predictive maintenance, connecting classical machine-learning methods with large language model applications.
  • Engineered reproducible data-processing pipelines (Python, SQL) supporting controlled experimentation, model evaluation, and performance monitoring over high-volume time series (10M+ records/day).

Data Scientist

Feb 2023 &ndash; Feb 2025

Clínica Dra. Nahid & Dr. Masoud, Valencia, Spain

  • Developed predictive models (scikit-learn) for patient-outcome forecasting, applying feature engineering, statistical modeling, and cross-validation to structured and unstructured clinical data.
  • Conducted exploratory and statistical analysis of healthcare datasets to identify clinically meaningful patterns and quantify predictive signal.
  • Communicated analytical findings to clinical collaborators through reproducible visualizations (Power BI, Tableau).

Latest Updates

Our paper on Lehmer Activation Units was published in Entropy (MDPI)!
Jan 2026
Joined Kinectrics as a Machine Learning Scientist (Co-op), working on deep-learning anomaly detection for wind-farm time series.
2025
Two manuscripts on exact neural-network optimization and financial volatility submitted and released on arXiv.
2025
Started my MSc in Data Science at the University of Colorado Boulder.
